author: writer-2

authorized_by: maintainer approval 2026-07-11 on issue #5 ('Approve all, d included'), approving the full a5-llm Gate-A REFUTE remediation recommended in docs/next/a5-llm-refute-adjudication.md (decision-list row 2: issue correction/2); execution delegated to the Fable role per the correction/1 precedent (docs/next/opus-execution-practices.md practice 4, Opus never concludes)

defect.statement: registry/verdicts/a5-llm.json field scale_language_licensed = "slope" is a verdict-GENERATION defect. MEASURED: tools/registry/verdict-gen.py (pre-fix line 560) computed the license purely from the count of distinct config.rung labels across eligible runs — license_ = "none" if len(rungs) < 2 else ("sign-only" if len(rungs) == 2 else "slope") — consulting NEITHER the pre-registered gates object NOR the frozen extrapolation_envelope_verbatim. For a5-llm the eligible runs carry rung labels {R0, R1, R2, R3}, so len(rungs)=4 emitted "slope".
defect.class: verdict-generator code defect (claim-governance metadata over-license); per-record, threefold: (1) R0 — the engine/abstain-all/answer-all baseline cells — is NOT a comparable model-scale rung and inflated the count; (2) the generator was blind to the licensing preconditions of docs/research-plan/08-stats-and-extrapolation.md section 2.1 (>=3 COMPARABLE rungs carrying the same pre-registered endpoint AND a validly produced scale-trend result — a5-llm's registered trend machinery produced NO valid result); (3) the generator was blind to the frozen envelope, which for a5-llm expressly says 'never a slope law'
defect.not_a_law_change: The G-12 count map (docs/research-plan/02-data-and-reporting.md:664; 1 rung -> none, 2 -> sign-only, >=3 -> slope) and 08-stats section 2.1 are UNCHANGED and are NOT the defect: programme law licenses a scale-trend fit at >=3 comparable rungs, and the generator implemented the LETTER of G-12. The defect is per-record enforcement, not licensing law. Explicitly rejected: any global 'slope requires >=4 rungs' rule. The defect attribution is to verdict-gen.py:560 + the failed gates + the frozen envelope — NOT to 08-stats section 2.3 (its C-6 note makes a 3-rung parametric prediction interval weak, not prohibited).
evidence: MEASURED: registry/experiments/a5-llm.json (frozen_sha256 11c3dc4e797bcaf12f199217bb3279e794882113a7feca530b36b2c3916631ad) extrapolation_envelope_verbatim: 'ONE model family, ONE vendor, 3 rungs => SIGN plus at most direction+order-of-magnitude trend language, never a slope law'; design.scale_rungs includes R0, whose cells are the engine/abstain-all/answer-all baselines, not model-scale rungs

MEASURED: reports/auto/a5-llm/analysis-output.json (sha256 f80a491e674c50e375a76dc31652bb189391b0b0c0163182cf520af63a997559): /gates/separation_valid = false and /analysis/holm/scale_trend_rag = false — the pre-registered sec-scale-trend member left the Holm family INSTRUMENT-INVALID before any p-value comparison (separation_gap = -0.1279), so NO licensed scale-trend fit exists for a 'slope' license to describe

MEASURED: the contradiction is self-exhibiting inside one artifact: reports/auto/a5-llm/verdict-a5-llm.md line 33 restates 'never a slope law' while line 220 prints 'Scale language licensed: **slope**'

MEASURED: the 2026-07-11 Gate-A re-audit (poc/audits/a5-llm/gate-a-rerun-20260711/last-message.json, outcome REFUTE) flags scale_language_assessment.is_defect=true; the adjudication concurs and disputes nothing in the audit

binding_reading.ruling: DECISION: the binding reading of a5-llm's scale_language_licensed is 'sign-only'. Basis, per the frozen instruments (min-rule): (1) the frozen envelope ceiling is sign-only ('never a slope law', verbatim in the verdict object itself); (2) only three comparable model-scale rungs exist (SmolLM2 R1/R2/R3; R0 is not a model-scale rung); (3) the registered scale-trend instrument failed (separation_valid=false, scale_trend_rag instrument-invalid), so no valid trend result exists. Downstream documents must not state ANY slope or scaling-law claim from a5-llm; the licensed scale statement is a SIGN at R1-R3 SmolLM2 plus at most direction+order-of-magnitude trend language, per the envelope.
binding_reading.no_measured_number_affected: MEASURED: no endpoint, gate boolean, Holm outcome, or fired verdict rule changes — the license field is claim-governance metadata; the numerical verdict rule reproduces independently (2026-07-11 audit: checks.verdict_rule=ok, fired rule index 2 -> PASS-PENDING-AUDIT). Every verdict-bearing number (primary 0.6601842374616171, cost_ratio_min 22835.94771419218, engine_matches_a5=1) was reproduced by the auditor to full float precision.

corrective_action.record_mutation: NONE. registry/experiments/a5-llm.json, results-log/a5-llm.jsonl, registry/verdicts/a5-llm.json and registry/frozen-index.json stand byte-identical as issued (append-never-edit). This erratum record is the binding correction of the license field until the verdict object is lawfully regenerated.
corrective_action.generator_fix: tools/registry/verdict-gen.py is fixed in the same change-set (per-record min-rule: machine-readable frozen ceiling design.scale_language_max AND >=3 measured rungs inside design.model_scale_rungs AND all design.scale_trend_valid_metrics pointers true in the analysis output; fail closed on any absent field). Regenerating the a5-llm verdict with the fixed generator yields sign-only. Sequencing per the adjudication section 2.2: the fix MUST be in place before any S7 regeneration of the a5-llm verdict; this correction governs the record in the interim, and any regeneration follows the maintainer's decision-1b ruling on verdict-update semantics with content-addressed traceability of the superseded object (old sha256 f893cf4752389e15c72d881ac779f30677aa25e5db48796acadace7ae0e7945e).
corrective_action.verdict_impact: registry/verdicts/a5-llm.json PASS-PENDING-AUDIT stands; a5-llm is NOT currently audit-confirmed (the 2026-07-11 REFUTE post-dates and invalidates the completeness of the 2026-07-10 CONFIRM). Path to CONFIRMED: Gate-A re-audit with registry/corrections/a5-llm/ (both records) and the corrected licensing rule in scope. Until then every positive a5-llm result remains MEASURED-UNAUDITED per programme law.
corrective_action.sibling_record: registry/corrections/f2/2-scale-license-erratum.json applies the analogous downgrade to f2 (different failure mode: genuine model-scale rungs, but envelope-capped to direction-only and no valid trend fit; verdict FAIL, nothing leans on it).
